title: Pentagon Seeks $200 Billion for Campaign Against Iran
summary:
Sheila Kahyaoglu, Managing Director in Equity Research at Jeffries says Department of Defense officials say missiles are the biggest growth area for the defense market. 

 The Pentagon has asked for an additional $200 billion from Congress to pay for the war against Iran, a person familiar with the matter said, as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth argued that the campaign was ahead of schedule and disputed that the US was getting embroiled in a quagmire.

title: BMO US President Sees 'Huge Opportunity' for Growth
summary: BMO US President Aron Levine discusses continued expansion into California, market uncertainty, and a new US growth strategy. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Israel Will Avoid Attacking Iran’s Energy Assets, Netanyahu Says
summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday said Israel would avoid future attacks on Iran’s energy infrastructure, after a pair of strikes on key Middle East gas operations sent energy prices soaring.

Oil Ends Choppy Day as Trump, Netanyahu Try to Ease War Concerns
summary: Oil prices bounced around in volatile trading Thursday, as damage to major Persian Gulf energy facilities overshadowed remarks from the US and Israeli leaders aimed at reassuring markets.

title: Israel Will Not Strike Iranian Energy Sites After Trump Rebuke
summary: Israel said it will no longer target energy infrastructure after an attack on an Iranian gas field sparked retaliatory strikes against energy assets across the Middle East, causing oil and gas prices to surge and prompting a rebuke from President Donald Trump.
